data.title: North Korean Hackers Launder $140M of $1.46B Stolen from Bybit
data.description:
Cryptocurrency exchange Bybit has suffered a significant blow, with a hack resulting in the theft of approximately $1.46 billion. As the recovery efforts commence, it has been revealed that North Korean hackers are involved in a laundering operation, further complicating the situation.

According to blockchain intelligence firms Elliptic and Arkham Intelligence, the hackers have begun laundering around $140 million (10%) of the stolen funds through anonymous exchange services and conversion to Bitcoin. This marks the beginning of what is expected to be a lengthy recovery process for the largest theft in crypto history.

The hackers distributed the stolen assets across 50 different wallets immediately after the theft, with each wallet containing approximately 10,000 ETH. These wallets are now being systematically emptied as the funds undergo conversion to Bitcoin. The attackers began by converting stolen tokens such as stETH and cmETH to Ethereum using decentralized exchanges, aligning with the typical methods employed by the Lazarus Group.

Both Elliptic and Arkham Intelligence have connected the attack to North Korea’s Lazarus Group, citing the use of decentralized exchanges and other services, including cross-chain bridges and coin swap services. The group has stolen over $3 billion in crypto assets since 2017, with proceeds reportedly funding North Korea’s ballistic missile program.

In response to the theft, Bybit announced early Saturday that it would offer a bounty of 10% of recovered funds—up to $140 million—to any on-chain security experts who assist in recovering the assets. This announcement came as the exchange faced mounting pressure from user withdrawals. Data from Arkham Intelligence shows that users have withdrawn approximately 23,000 BTC from Bybit’s hot wallet since the incident, representing an outflow of roughly $1.7 billion since Friday afternoon.

The anonymous crypto exchange eXch has emerged as a key player in the laundering operation, processing tens of millions of dollars in stolen assets despite direct requests from Bybit to block the activity. In a purported email response, eXch claimed it chose not to acknowledge Bybit’s requests due to past reputational conflicts between the two entities.

A coordinated industry response has led to the freezing of $42.85 million in stolen funds across multiple platforms. THORChain has blacklisted several addresses linked to the North Korean hack

data.title: Oil Prices Fall on Trade Concerns, Push for Russia-Ukraine Peace Deal
data.description:
Oil prices have been on a rollercoaster ride in recent months, with geopolitical tensions and trade concerns driving market sentiment. As the world watches the potential for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal, oil prices have taken a dip, reflecting investors' cautious optimism. This article explores the factors contributing to the recent decline in oil prices and the potential implications for the global energy market.



Trade Concerns Weigh on Oil Demand

The ongoing trade tensions, particularly between the United States and China, have been a significant headwind for global oil demand. The threat of a prolonged trade war has sparked demand fears, pushing oil prices down. According to a report by the International Monetary Fund (IMF), the U.S.-China trade war could lower global GDP by 0.8% in 2019, which in turn reduces oil demand and puts downward pressure on prices.

Russia-Ukraine Peace Deal: A Double-Edged Sword for Oil Prices

The potential for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal has added another bearish geopolitical factor for oil prices this year. If talks result in a deal and a possible sanctions relief on Moscow's crude oil and petroleum product exports, oil prices could ease by up to $10 per barrel for the Brent benchmark, according to Bank of America. This could have a significant impact on the global economy, as it would lower energy costs for consumers and businesses.

However, the easing of sanctions on Russian oil exports could also depress refining margins globally amid higher diesel supply out of Russia. A $10 per barrel reduction in oil prices could seriously disincentivize U.S. drillers, potentially leading to a decrease in U.S. oil production. This could further impact global oil supply and prices.



Market Sentiment and the Role of Algorithmic Trading

Market participants are realizing that, although the Trump administration won't be able to meaningfully lower prices from drill-baby-drill, they certainly won't pursue a policy agenda that proactively raises oil prices vis-à-vis sanctions. The decline in oil prices has been exacerbated by algorithmic traders dumping bullish positions after futures breached the $75 price range. Quant funds are also deleveraging amid the market decline, contributing to selling activity.



Conclusion

The recent decline in oil prices can be attributed to a combination of trade concerns, the potential for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal, and market sentiment driven by algorithmic trading. While a peace deal could lead to eased access to Russian oil supply, it could also disincentivize U.S. drillers and impact OPEC+'s plans to return more supply to the market. As top U.S. and Russian officials meet to discuss the possible end of the war, the market will be closely watching the potential implications for global oil supply and prices. Investors should remain vigilant and adapt their strategies accordingly to navigate the volatile energy market landscape.

data.title: XRP Surges Amid Bitcoin's Crypto ETP Rout
data.description:
Cryptocurrency investment products experienced significant fluctuations last week, with Bitcoin exchange-traded products (ETPs) facing steep losses as investor sentiment turned wary. Despite the negative trend in cryptocurrency ETPs, XRP has emerged as a relative bright spot, attracting considerable inflows.

Last week, crypto ETPs suffered dramatic outflows led by Bitcoin while XRP attracted investments amid investor caution, signaling a shift in market dynamics. In the latest trading week, Bitcoin investment products took a significant hit, resulting in outflows totaling $571 million. This drastic decline illustrates the ongoing challenges faced by the largest cryptocurrency as it remains vulnerable to market fluctuations.

Conversely, altcoin ETPs displayed resilience, with XRP ETPs emerging as a leader in investor interest, garnering $38 million in inflows. This trend highlights a possible shift in investor focus toward assets perceived as undervalued or with favorable regulatory prospects. Notably, XRP ETPs have accumulated a remarkable $819 million in inflows since November 2024, underscoring growing optimism regarding the anticipated resolution of the SEC’s Ripple lawsuit, which could pave the way for the approval of a spot XRP ETF.

Other altcoins also showed positive movements, with Solana (SOL), Ether (ETH), and Sui (SUI) recording inflows of $8.9 million, $3.7 million, and $1.5 million, respectively, signaling increased diversification among investor portfolios.

In a surprising turn of events, BlackRock’s iShares exchange-traded funds (ETFs) faced a collective loss of $22 million over the past week. This anomaly in their usually robust performance underscores the volatility inherent in today’s market. However, ProShares ETFs demonstrated a contrary trend, seeing inflows of $38 million, reflecting investor preferences for strategies that might mitigate risks in a turbulent economic environment.

The recent trends observed in crypto ETP flows indicate a critical rethinking among investors. As Bitcoin continues to grapple with outflows, the interest pivoting towards XRP and altcoins could signify a broader diversification strategy within crypto investments. Additionally, ongoing regulatory developments will play a crucial role in shaping future inflows and overall market sentiment.

data.title: Protiviti: A Beacon of Excellence in Consulting
data.description:
Protiviti, a global consulting firm, has once again been recognized as a Best Place to Work, this time securing a spot on Glassdoor's prestigious 2025 list. This accolade is a testament to the company's unwavering commitment to fostering a positive work environment, offering professional development opportunities, and valuing its employees. In this article, we will delve into the specific aspects of Protiviti's culture and employee experience that contribute to its high Glassdoor rating and explore how other companies can replicate or improve upon these aspects.



Protiviti's commitment to diversity, inclusion, and work-life balance plays a significant role in its employees' satisfaction and productivity. The company's diversity score of 'B' on Comparably places it in the top 25% of similar-sized companies, indicating that it is actively promoting diversity among its employees. Diverse employees at Protiviti have rated Manager, Outlook, and Leadership as the highest categories they have scored, suggesting that the company's inclusive culture fosters a positive work environment. Women at Protiviti have also rated Manager, Outlook, and Leadership highly, with a score of 'B-', which places the company in the top 40% of similar-sized companies on Comparably. This demonstrates that Protiviti is committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees, regardless of gender.



Protiviti's commitment to work-life balance is evident in its flexible work arrangements, such as a work-from-home hybrid model, which contributes to employee satisfaction and happiness at work. The company's parental leave policies, which are gender-neutral and offer up to ten weeks of fully paid leave, demonstrate Protiviti's commitment to supporting employees' personal lives. In Australia, new parents and caregivers have the option of flexi-time and additional leave to help them balance home and work life. These initiatives not only contribute to employee satisfaction but also help Protiviti attract and retain top talent.

Protiviti's commitment to diversity, inclusion, and work-life balance influences employee satisfaction and productivity, ultimately driving long-term benefits for the company. A positive work environment, fostered by these initiatives, leads to higher employee satisfaction and engagement, which can result in increased productivity, creativity, and innovation. A diverse and inclusive workforce can bring a broader range of perspectives and ideas to the table, leading to better decision-making and problem-solving. Protiviti's commitment to work-life balance can help the company attract and retain top talent, as employees value the flexibility and support offered by the organization. This can lead to a more stable and skilled workforce, reducing recruitment and training costs in the long run.

In conclusion, Protiviti's commitment to diversity, inclusion, and work-life balance plays a crucial role in fostering a positive work environment, enhancing employee satisfaction and productivity, and driving long-term business benefits. The company's recognition as a Best Place to Work and its various awards for diversity and inclusion further validate the positive impact of these initiatives on its employees and overall success. By focusing on these aspects of Protiviti's culture and employee experience, other companies can strive to create a similarly positive work environment that attracts and retains top talent.

data.title: Equitable Boosts AllianceBernstein Stake with Reinsurance Deal Funds
data.description:
Equitable Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: EQH) has announced plans to use funds from a recent reinsurance deal to increase its stake in AllianceBernstein (AB), a leading global investment management firm. The transaction, which involves the reinsurance of a significant portion of Equitable's variable annuity liabilities, will generate approximately $1.2 billion of value on a Statutory basis. This includes an expected $800 million capital release, a positive ceding commission and consideration for the acquisition of Corporate Solutions Life Re totaling approximately $300 million subject to adjustment, and approximately $100 million in tax benefits.

The reinsurance agreement with Venerable Holdings, Inc. (Venerable) will significantly de-risk Equitable's balance sheet, reducing its required capital by 64% and increasing its Risk-Based Capital (RBC) ratio by approximately 60 points. The transaction will also unlock statutory capital of approximately $1.2 billion, consistent with the Company's economic valuation. Equitable will continue to administer the reinsured policies, ensuring a seamless client service experience and maintaining control over the policyholder relationship.



Equitable's decision to increase its stake in AllianceBernstein aligns with its long-term business objectives, including expanding its investment management capabilities, growing its asset under management, and enhancing its distribution network. By investing in AB, Equitable gains access to a broader range of investment management services and products, diversifying its revenue streams and mitigating risks associated with relying solely on traditional insurance products.

AllianceBernstein's extensive distribution network and client base also provide Equitable with an opportunity to expand its reach and tap into new markets. This increased distribution capability helps Equitable to grow its customer base and generate more revenue. Additionally, the integration of AllianceBernstein's operations with Equitable's existing business can achieve synergies and cost savings, leading to improved operational efficiency and reduced expenses.



Equitable's strategic move to boost its stake in AllianceBernstein is expected to generate attractive returns on capital, as mentioned by Leslie Barbi, Executive Vice President, Chief Investment Officer, RGA. The investment in Ruby Reinsurance Company (Ruby Re), a reinsurance sidecar vehicle sponsored by RGA and focused on the U.S. asset-intensive market, represents a significant milestone in AB's continued goal of leadership within insurance asset management.

In conclusion, Equitable's use of funds from the reinsurance deal to boost its stake in AllianceBernstein is a strategic move that aligns with its long-term business objectives. The transaction de-risks Equitable's balance sheet, reduces required capital, and increases its RBC ratio. By investing in AB, Equitable gains access to diversified revenue streams, enhanced distribution capabilities, synergies and cost savings, talent attraction and retention, and alignment with its long-term business objectives. This strategic move positions Equitable to better compete in the evolving financial services landscape.

data.title: BlackSky Soars: Tech Giant's Swift Recovery
Equifax's 2024 Earnings: EPS Misses Expectations, but Cloud Migration and Acquisitions Drive Growth
data.description:
Equifax, the global leader in consumer credit reporting, reported its full-year 2024 earnings on February 24, 2025, with earnings per share (EPS) missing analyst expectations. Despite this, the company's revenue growth and strategic initiatives, such as cloud migration and acquisitions, have driven its financial performance and future prospects. Let's delve into the key factors contributing to Equifax's EPS miss and explore the strategic initiatives that have positioned the company for growth.

Equifax's EPS missed analyst expectations primarily due to a decline in operating margin, rising costs, high debt load, legal proceedings, and economic sensitivity. The company's operating margin declined from 20.6% in 2022 to 18.3% in 2024, signaling rising costs outpacing revenue growth. This decline in profitability negatively impacted EPS. Equifax's high debt load of $5.0 billion, with $688 million due within a year, increases financial risk and sensitivity to interest rate changes. Ongoing legal proceedings and government investigations, including CFPB inquiries and Canadian class actions related to the 2017 cybersecurity incident, represent potential liabilities and reputational risks. Equifax's sensitivity to economic conditions, particularly in the mortgage market, could pressure revenue and earnings in the future. The expectation of weaker U.S. mortgage credit activity in 2025 could further impact earnings.



Despite the EPS miss, Equifax's revenue growth in different segments contributed to the overall earnings performance. The Workforce Solutions segment's revenue grew by 7% in the fourth quarter of 2024, driven by strong 10% Verification Services revenue growth led by the Government and Mortgage businesses. The USIS segment's revenue grew by over 10% in the fourth quarter, led by very strong 47% Mortgage revenue growth. The International segment's revenue grew by 11% on a local currency basis and 3% on a reported basis, with Latin America leading the growth with a 29% increase in revenue on a local currency basis.

Equifax's strategic initiatives, such as cloud migration and acquisitions, have significantly impacted its financial performance and future prospects. The company's strategic focus on cloud migration reshaped its technology infrastructure, aiming to enhance agility and innovation. By the end of 2024, close to 85% of the company's revenue was generated from the new EFX Cloud. This migration led to a 12% increase in the New Product Vitality Index, demonstrating the success of leveraging new cloud capabilities for product innovation. The cloud transformation is expected to boost innovation and efficiency, driving growth in 2025 and beyond, and delivering higher margins and accelerating free cash flow in the future.

Equifax's acquisition of Boa Vista Serviços (BVS) in Latin America was a key driver of international growth. In 2024, Latin America led the region with 32% revenue growth, boosted by the BVS acquisition. The BVS acquisition demonstrates Equifax's commitment to international expansion, particularly in Latin America, but integration and FX risks need careful management. Successful execution of these acquisitions is crucial for long-term competitiveness, as they position Equifax to capitalize on growing market needs and potentially create stronger moats.



In conclusion, Equifax's EPS miss in 2024 was primarily driven by a decline in operating margin, rising costs, high debt load, legal proceedings, and economic sensitivity. Despite this, the company's revenue growth in different segments and strategic initiatives, such as cloud migration and acquisitions, have positioned Equifax for future growth. As investors, we must remain vigilant and monitor Equifax's progress in managing its costs, reducing financial risk, addressing legal and reputational issues, and adapting to economic changes. By doing so, we can capitalize on the company's strategic initiatives and potential growth opportunities.

data.title: 5 Fundamentally Solid Stocks That Are Oversold and Poised for Impulsive Rally (With Bonus Sell List)
data.description:
The sharp decline in the U.S. stock market last week has pushed risk sentiment to levels last seen on January 13th. At that time, stocks completed a correction, and the S&P 500 rebounded 5% within a week. While it's uncertain if the current round of sell-offs has ended, many fundamentally solid stocks have reached extremely oversold levels, positioning them for a potential short-term dead cat bounce. These stocks could serve as a hedge against further downside risk and may significantly outperform during a market recovery—whether driven by technical or fundamental factors. We've also included a list of stocks showing warning signs as a bonus at the end of the article, making them potential candidates for short positions.

Amazon (AMZN)

Amazon has dropped to the December 4th price level, a critical resistance point. Just last month, shares hovered around $216 before bouncing back. The stock has now corrected nearly 10% since its latest earnings, driven by a combination of disappointing guidance and valuation concerns. Despite the negative sentiment, the RSI has fallen to 16.8, a level last seen on August 5th, when a significant sell-off also coincided with a sharp oversold bounce to new highs. Given these factors, Amazon is in our buy-the-dip list, poised for a potential rebound in the short term, even if broader market conditions remain weak.

The Trade Desk (TTD)

The Trade Desk is another stock facing extreme pessimism. After a 40% drop over two weeks, driven by Q4 revenue and guidance misses, investor sentiment is at a low point. However, as a key supplier to Netflix and an emerging player in AI-powered advertising, The Trade Desk's fundamentals remain solid. Its RSI has now fallen to 10.2, last seen in November 2022, when the stock rallied 30% in just one week. The trading volume has also been down for five consecutive days, indicating reduced selling pressure. Given the low RSI and solid fundamentals, we expect a sharp bounce soon.

Walmart (WMT)

Walmart faced a significant 2-day sell-off after issuing weaker-than-expected guidance, citing tariff risks and a conservative outlook. Despite this, we view it as another buy-the-dip opportunity. The stock had previously surged as investors viewed it as a safe haven asset with strong pricing power. After the nearly 9% drop from pre-earnings levels, more funds are likely to flow into Walmart as a hedge against uncertainty. The RSI has fallen to 20, not extremely oversold but the lowest level in 10 months. If it continues to decline, more investors may step in to buy, given the company's robust consumer spending and self-reliance.

Caterpillar (CAT)

Caterpillar's stock has been negatively impacted by concerns over the U.S. economic outlook. The stock has retreated to September levels, but global manufacturing is still growing, as evidenced by a rise in global manufacturing PMI to 51.6 in February. Caterpillar remains well-positioned to mitigate tariff risks due to its status as a net exporter. The stock has an RSI of 13, not its lowest level historically but still showing oversold conditions. As economic prospects improve, Caterpillar, as a key player in global manufacturing, should benefit from a recovery in demand.

Block (XYZ)

Block saw its shares plunge 18% on its worst trading day after Q4 earnings missed expectations. However, the company's core business remains strong, with solid payment volume and EBITDA results. Despite missing revenue and earnings targets, the company reiterated its 2025 guidance. The RSI has dropped to 7.2, an extremely oversold level reminiscent of September 25, 2023. While the rebound may not be as swift or as large as The Trade Desk's, the stock is worth considering for a buy on the dip due to its strong fundamentals, though the bounce may take longer to materialize.

Bonus: Sell List (with Brief Comments)

Alibaba: RSI at 94, rally momentum fading; good news already priced in, hedge funds likely to cash out.

PDD: Faces Trump's tariff risks; rally driven by optimism around China AI but lacks substantial AI features.

Broadcom: MA (3, 7, 10) turning downward; lower highs forming on the chart.

Tesla: Musk's heavy political involvement raises concerns; AI and robotaxi take time, earnings warning possible, limited business benefits under Trump due to conflict-of-interest risks.

Uber: MA (3) at an inflection point; lacks momentum to surpass October's highs and may face further struggles.

data.title: XRP, Remittix, and Ethereum: The Crypto Market's Biggest Battles
data.description:
Cryptocurrency markets are abuzz with speculation about the future of XRP, Remittix, and Ethereum (ETH). XRP enthusiasts are eagerly awaiting regulatory clarity and expanded partnerships to drive the token's price higher. Some experts predict a breakout if the SEC case is resolved favorably, while others foresee triple-digit values by 2025 if financial institutions fully embrace RippleNet.

Meanwhile, a new contender, Remittix, has caught investors' attention by bridging crypto with everyday banking. With a presale that has already raised over $12.7 million, Remittix aims to transform cross-border payments by enabling users to send crypto that converts instantly into local fiat. This direct fiat integration could drive widespread adoption, especially in underbanked regions, and some foresee Remittix hitting $1 within a year.

Ethereum (ETH) remains in focus as analysts wonder when it could reclaim $4,000. Bulls suggest that if Ethereum's scaling upgrades reduce gas fees and draw more dApps and institutional funds, mainstream adoption might increase, paving the way for big price jumps. However, a push above $4,000 requires a fresh wave of DeFi enthusiasm and a larger crypto market rebound.

As the SEC case edges toward resolution, XRP Price Prediction discussions may intensify. Ethereum's path to $4,000 depends on broader market revival and scaling progress. Meanwhile, Remittix stands poised to carve out its own niche, leveraging tangible cross-border solutions that overshadow mere speculation. Investors seeking real-world adoption might find Remittix's presale compelling, particularly if it cements a foothold as the next big payment-focused altcoin.

data.title: Bybit Invests $1M in EthicHub to Empower Small Farmers
data.description:
Bybit, the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume, has invested $1 million in EthicHub, a blockchain-based platform dedicated to providing fair financial services to small farmers. This strategic investment is part of Bybit's commitment to the Blockchain for Good Alliance (BGA), an initiative aimed at harnessing the power of blockchain technology to drive positive social change.

EthicHub, a standout project supported by the BGA Incubation Programme, has been recognized for its mission to connect people with fair financial services. At the BGA Web3 Oscar event, EthicHub won the Grand Social Impact Award, further cementing its status as a leader in ethical lending practices. With Bybit's financial support, EthicHub will evolve into a platform that extends better financial services to small coffee growers, enabling them to secure fair loans and boost their productivity.

The investment from Bybit will establish the "Bybit Pool," a dedicated funding mechanism within EthicHub. This fund will provide small and medium-sized coffee farmers with equal borrowing opportunities, allowing them to secure fair loans without resorting to exorbitant interest rates from traditional financial institutions. Bybit's use of blockchain technology in this initiative aims to create economic opportunities for farmers and support rural communities.

The investment will remain within the EthicHub platform for continuous monitoring, with LendingDev, operating as EthicHub's company, providing performance reporting on the fund's progress three times each quarter. Bybit will receive an 8% annual payment for the next 15 months, ensuring a mutually beneficial partnership. Through this collaboration, Bybit believes it can help people without access to regular banking services, demonstrating that blockchain can generate both social benefit and growth.

EthicHub's team has highlighted the potential impact of the funds, stating that they will enable coffee farmers to secure better financial support, reduce farming wastages, and access bigger markets. Small coffee farmers have long struggled with unfavorable prices and lending practices, but ethical blockchain lending has begun to address these challenges. Bybit's investment will benefit many farmers who previously lacked financial security, allowing them to mechanize their businesses, invest in sustainable farming, and sell their coffee in global markets.

data.title: "Crypto Networking: Your Key to Industry Success"
data.description:
Building a robust network in the crypto industry is crucial for success, as it can open doors to opportunities and partnerships that might otherwise remain elusive. Even with exceptional skills or innovative ideas, having the right connections can make or break a crypto project. This strategic guide offers practical tips to help you organically grow your network within the crypto community.

First, it's essential to embrace a mindset shift and become comfortable with putting yourself out there. Networking events can be intimidating, but pushing past initial nerves can lead to valuable connections. Instead of focusing on selling, approach conversations with genuine curiosity and a desire to learn about others. Remember that networking is a two-way street, and adding value to others can foster mutually beneficial relationships.

Attending both online and in-person crypto events is another effective way to build connections. Large conferences like Consensus, as well as smaller local meetups and university crypto clubs, can provide opportunities to meet key players in the industry. Additionally, virtual events allow you to expand your network globally without significant upfront expenditure.

Strategically growing your crypto social media presence can also help you build connections. Clean up your Twitter profile and engage with key players in the crypto space. Publish educational content on LinkedIn to establish yourself as a thought leader, and join invite-only groups focused on blockchain projects, NFTs, DeFi, and investing to have 1:1 conversations with experts.

Connecting with crypto founders and investors is vital when fundraising or searching for investment deals. Look up founders of crypto projects on LinkedIn and reach out politely, expressing interest in their projects or proposing a meeting. Connect with crypto VCs by looking for Partners, Analysts, and Associates focused on crypto investments at VC and investment funds. Pay attention to crypto connections in common, and ask for warm introductions from mutual connections.

As you build your network, it's crucial to follow up regularly with your expanding crypto network. Manage contacts in a CRM to track how you met and when you last spoke, and set reminders to follow up regularly. Connect on social media, especially LinkedIn, to stay at the top of their minds, and add new contacts to targeted email nurture tracks to share relevant articles, tools, and offers based on their interests.

Finally, be patient and persistent in your networking efforts. Authentic relationships and genuine commitment to moving the blockchain ecosystem forward can lead to unexpected opportunities. Don't expect immediate

data.title: "Whales Accumulate 1M LTC as Litecoin ETF Approval Looms"
data.description:
Litecoin (LTC) has seen a significant increase in interest from large-scale investors, known as "whales," who have accumulated nearly one million LTC tokens in the past two weeks. This buying activity coincides with a high probability of Litecoin ETF approval by 2025, as estimated by industry experts.

The cryptocurrency is currently trading at $127.97, with a slight 0.46% decline over the past 24 hours. Despite this minor dip, major investors continue to show strong interest in accumulating Litecoin, indicating a bullish sentiment among large-scale investors.

Crypto analyst Ali Martinez reported the whale activity, noting that these large-scale purchases occurred within a two-week timeframe. This type of buying behavior often signals that wealthy investors expect positive price movement in the future.

The timing of these purchases aligns with recent developments in the ETF space. Canary Capital’s Litecoin ETF has been listed on the Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC), representing a step forward in the preparation process for a possible launch.

Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas has placed the odds of a Litecoin ETF approval at 90%. This estimate exceeds the already optimistic prediction from Polymarket data, which shows an 85% probability of approval by 2025. However, Balchunas added an important note of caution: while the DTCC listing shows preparation for a potential launch, it does not guarantee that regulators will approve the ETF.

The high probability of approval places Litecoin among several other cryptocurrencies expected to receive ETF approval in 2025, including Solana, Dogecoin, and Cardano. This suggests a broader trend toward altcoin ETF launches, which could bring new money into the respective markets.

A Litecoin ETF would create a new way for traditional investors to gain exposure to the cryptocurrency through regular brokerage accounts, making it more accessible to a wider range of investors. ETFs typically make it easier for institutional investors to include an asset in their portfolios by removing the technical challenges of buying and storing cryptocurrency directly.

The whale buying activity represents roughly 1 million LTC tokens added to large wallets. This amount of purchasing can often lead to price increases if the supply of available tokens for sale decreases. Traditional

data.title: Iraq Minister Hopes Kurdistan Oil Exports to Resume in Two Days
data.description:
In a significant development, the Iraqi Minister of Oil, Ihsan Abdul Jabbar, has expressed optimism that oil exports from the Kurdistan Region of Iraq (KRI) will resume within the next two days. This announcement comes after a nearly two-year halt in exports following a ruling by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) in favor of Iraq in an arbitration case over the independent export of oil by the Kurdish Regional Government (KRG).

The resumption of oil exports from Kurdistan is a crucial step in addressing the long-standing dispute between the KRG and the Iraqi federal government over oil and gas revenues. The KRG had been exporting oil independently through an independent pipeline to the Turkish port of Ceyhan, which Baghdad labeled as "smuggling" and "robbery." The ICC's ruling in March 2023 led to the halt of oil exports from the Kurdish region, disrupting the KRG's revenue and exacerbating tensions between the two parties.

The Iraqi oil ministry has stated that the necessary procedures have been completed to restart shipments through Turkey's Ceyhan port. Exports will resume in accordance with federal budget regulations and Iraq's OPEC production quota, according to an "agreed-upon framework." The ministry has urged the Kurdish region's authorities to transfer crude oil extracted from the region's oil fields to the State Organization for Marketing of Oil, facilitating its exports via the Iraq-Turkey pipeline.

The resumption of oil exports from Kurdistan is expected to have a positive impact on the region's economy, as oil exports are a crucial source of income for the KRG. However, it is essential to address the underlying political and security concerns to ensure long-term stability and prosperity in the region. The ongoing conflict between Turkey and the Kurdistan Workers' Party (PKK) has added complexity to the geopolitical landscape, and the stability of the Iraqi government will also play a crucial role in the future dynamics of the situation.

In conclusion, the resumption of oil exports from Kurdistan is a significant development that addresses a long-standing source of tension between the KRG and the Iraqi federal government. The Iraqi Minister of Oil's optimism regarding the resumption of exports within the next two days highlights the potential for improved relations and economic growth in the region. However, it is crucial to address the underlying political and security concerns to ensure long-term stability and prosperity in the region.

data.title: Saia Soars: Unlocking Growth Potential
Baidu's Ji Yue Auto: A Surprising 1.06% Premarket Boost!
NVIDIA Earnings Loom: A Make-or-Break Moment for AI and U.S. Markets
data.description:
As NVIDIA prepares to release its highly anticipated fourth-quarter earnings report for the fiscal year 2024, investors and tech enthusiasts are paying close attention to the global leader in graphics processors and pioneer in the artificial intelligence (AI) domain. Expectations for the quarter ending February 26 indicate revenue of $410 billion, slightly below the market consensus of $420 billion, with a significant slowdown in revenue growth to 73%.

NVIDIA's earnings report not only reflects its own performance but also serves as a barometer for the health of the entire AI industry. Analysts caution that should NVIDIA's performance or guidance fail to meet expectations, it could lead to widespread turbulence in the AI sector and even impact the stability of the U.S. stock market.

On the eve of NVIDIA's earnings announcement, there is already a sense of unease in the U.S. stock market. The VIX index, which measures market volatility, spiked on Friday, along with sharp declines across the major indices. Notably, hedge fund titan Steve Cohen issued a warning that U.S. equities could experience a pullback, with economic growth possibly slowing to 1.5%.

The focus of the earnings report will be on AI data center revenue growth, product transition within inventory, and perspectives on AI application deployment. Despite robust global demand for AI chips, NVIDIA's outlook is complicated by intensifying competition. Additionally, delays in the delivery of its Blackwell series chips have the potential to affect overall revenue and gross margin.

According to Morgan Stanley, should NVIDIA's revenue exceed expectations, its stock price may rise by 3% to 15%; conversely, if performance falls short, the stock could drop by 5% to 10%. It's important to note that since the advent of ChatGPT, NVIDIA has been a key driver of the S&P 500 index, and its earnings report will directly influence the market performance of related chip giants like TSMC and SK Hynix.

Overall, NVIDIA's earnings report is a critical indicator for the AI industry, with its future performance having significant implications for the technology market. This anticipation is coupled with high expectations and concerns among investors regarding NVIDIA's trajectory. For automakers and potential buyers alike, advancements in AI technology are keenly observed, as they will directly impact future innovations in electric vehicles, autonomous driving, and smart car features.

In summary, NVIDIA's imminent earnings release will not only reveal its market standings but will also prompt new reflections and expectations on technological advancements within the automotive industry. The market's response remains to be seen.

data.title: Why Buffett Holds a Record $334.2 Billion in Cash?
data.description:
Buffett is renowned for his stock-picking acumen, yet nowadays, he shows an increasing inclination towards holding cash. Two days ago, Berkshire Hathaway, the company under Buffett's leadership, released its fourth-quarter and full-year financial reports for 2024, accompanied by Buffett's annual letter to shareholders.

As of the end of 2024, the total amount of cash and cash equivalents held by this renowned investor's company, Berkshire Hathaway, reached a staggering $334.2 billion, setting a new historical high. The proportion of these assets in the company's portfolio is also the highest since 1998.

 

Maintaining a large cash reserve has always been a consistent practice of Berkshire. However, the continuous expansion of the cash reserve scale recently has drawn the attention of some market observers. By carefully studying Buffett's latest letter to shareholders, we can seek clues to understand the views of this 94-year-old on the stock market, as well as his perspectives on long-term strategies, market conditions, and diversified investment portfolios.

Where Does the Cash Come From?

One of the reasons for the cash accumulation is that Berkshire has sold a substantial amount of Apple stocks. In recent years, the valuation of Apple stocks has been much higher than when Buffett's company established its position from 2016 to 2018.

 

According to data from FactSet, starting from the end of 2023, Berkshire has reduced its shares in Apple for four consecutive quarters. In the fourth quarter of last year, Berkshire temporarily halted further sales of Apple shares.

This is because this portion of the position in Berkshire Hathaway's stock portfolio has already accounted for a significant proportion. When his designated successor, Greg Abel, eventually takes over the company, he will be responsible for this transition.

Some investors and analysts speculate that Buffett's conservative moves over the past year are not a judgment on the market but rather a preparation for future deployments by reducing overly large positions and accumulating cash. The massive amount of cash will provide the successor with room for trial and error, especially for this successor who has been hailed as a prophet by the world.

Historical experience shows that new management often engages in aggressive mergers and acquisitions due to performance pressure (such as Microsoft's acquisition of LinkedIn in the early days of Satya Nadella's tenure). However, Berkshire's $334.2 billion reserve is sufficient to offset the potential losses of any single transaction.

Why Hoard a Large Amount of Cash?

Buffett also seems to be well aware that his move of holding a large amount of cash has received significant attention from the market. In his letter to shareholders, he stated that although some commentators believe that Berkshire's cash position is extremely high, the vast majority of the funds are still invested in stocks, and this preference will not change.

Buffett emphasized, Berkshire's shareholders can rest assured that we will always invest the majority of our funds in stocks - mainly American enterprises, but many of these companies also have important businesses in the global market. Berkshire will never prefer to hold cash equivalents while ignoring the equity of high-quality enterprises, whether it is a controlling stake or a partial stake.

In fact, Berkshire's huge cash holdings have raised questions among shareholders and observers, especially in a situation where interest rates are expected to decline from multi-year highs. In recent years, the CEO and Chairman of Berkshire has expressed frustration about the expensive market and the scarcity of buying opportunities. Some investors and analysts have grown impatient with the lack of action and are seeking explanations.

1. The Valuation Trap

Long-time observers of Berkshire believe that the main reason why Buffett is hoarding cash is the currently high market valuation.

The current forward price-to-earnings ratio (P/E) of the S&P 500 is 22.3 times, which is a 20% premium compared to the 10-year average of 18.6 times. The valuation of technology stocks, in particular, deviates significantly from the historical median. In the fields where Buffett excels, it has become difficult to find bargains among high-quality large enterprises, making it challenging for Buffett to find investment targets that meet his investment criteria. Take Apple as an example; its P/E ratio exceeded 30 times at one point in 2024, prompting Buffett to continuously reduce his holdings to the current 28%. As Buffett emphasized in his letter, We rarely find ourselves in a position of opportunity, which is in line with his creed of being fearful when others are greedy. 

Among the industries Buffett is familiar with, the prices of the large, high-quality enterprises that the company is looking for have risen too high, and stock pickers cannot be confident that an investment will bring substantial returns to Berkshire and its shareholders.

Statistics from The Wall Street Journal show that Berkshire's return rate over the past decade (225%) has already lagged behind that of the S&P 500 (241%), confirming the dilemma of the curse of scale where it is difficult for large entities to perform well.

 

2. The Value Depression

In stark contrast to the US stock market, the five major Japanese trading houses (such as Itochu and Mitsubishi) that Berkshire has heavily invested in have an average P/E ratio of only 5-8 times, a dividend yield of 3%-6%, and have achieved arbitrage through zero-interest-rate bond financing in Japan. As of the end of 2024, this investment has a floating profit of $9.7 billion, and the annual dividend income reaches $812 million. This cross-market valuation gap reveals Buffett's logic of exchanging cash for time: shrinking the front line in the US stock market bubble and shifting to Asian assets with a greater margin of safety.

 

Moreover, it is likely that the investment in Japanese assets will continue to increase in the future, and his successor will also hold Japanese assets for decades to come.

3. The Cushion

This scale is reminiscent of the classic case during the 2008 financial crisis when Buffett injected $10 billion in preferred stock into Goldman Sachs, with an annual return rate of 17%. In the current environment of geopolitical turmoil and the intensifying risk of future AI regulation, the cash reserve is essentially a put option, reserving the initiative to capture asymmetric returns.

4. The Arbitrage Space at the End of the Interest Rate Cycle

Although the market's expectation of the Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts is on the rise, the yield of short - term US Treasury bonds still remains close to 4.5%. Based on the estimation of Berkshire's cash reserves in the first three quarters of 2024, the interest income alone exceeded $10 billion. In the context where the yield of low - risk assets is rarely higher than the dividend yield of the S&P 500 (1.5%), this cash cow model has become a practical choice for hedging against inflation.


The Dynamic Balance of Value Investing

Buffett reaffirmed in his letter, Berkshire will never prefer cash over the equity of high-quality enterprises. This statement echoes his argument during the Internet bubble in 1999 that cash is a call option. Although the current cash proportion has reached a historical peak, the growth in the value of non-listed controlled enterprises has offset the decline in stock holdings.

Compared with the preference for low-valuation assets in the 1970s (such as Berkshire Hathaway's textile factory), the current strategy places more emphasis on the stability of cash flow. The investment in Japanese trading houses reflects this shift: enterprises like Mitsubishi have a stable return on equity (ROE) of over 10% and have benefited from the dividend increase brought about by Japan's corporate governance reform. This combination of high dividend + low volatility is essentially an advanced substitute for cash.

Problems cannot be solved by hope; action must be taken - Buffett quoted Munger's maxim to explain the current strategy. This is similar to the history when the two of them remained inactive during the technology bubble in 2000 and eventually made a profit of $4 billion by bottom-fishing PetroChina in 2003.

Conclusion

Buffett's cash empire is essentially the ultimate response of a value investor to the market cycle. When the Buffett Indicator (the total market value of US stocks/GDP) breaks through the 200% warning line, this octogenarian has built an anti-fragile barrier with $334.2 billion in cash. This is both a long-term belief in the American Miracle and a calm hedge against short-term enthusiasm. As stated at the end of the letter, Berkshire's future will still be rooted in the United States, but our wallets will always be open to global value - in an era of valuation differentiation and order reconstruction, cash is not the end but the starting point of the next miracle.

data.title: Montana Rejects Bitcoin Investment Bill
data.description: Montana's House of Representatives has rejected a bill that would have allowed the state to invest public funds in Bitcoin and other digital assets. The decision, made on February 22, 2025, marks the latest development in a growing nationwide debate over state-level Bitcoin reserve initiatives.House Bill 429, introduced by Representative Curtis Schomer, failed to pass with a vote of 41-59. The proposed legislation would have created a special revenue account permitting investments of up to $50 million in cryptocurrencies, stablecoins, and precious metals. The bill specified that eligible digital assets must maintain an average market capitalization above $750 billion over the previous calendar year. Currently, Bitcoin is the only cryptocurrency meeting this criterion, with a market cap of $1.8 trillion.During the House Floor Session, several lawmakers expressed concerns about the risks associated with cryptocurrency investments. Representative Steven Kelly emphasized the responsibility to protect taxpayer money, while Representative Bill Mercer opposed giving the Montana Board of Investments discretion to invest in cryptocurrencies and non-fungible tokens.Some representatives supported the bill, arguing it could benefit taxpayers. Representative Lee Demming advocated for maximizing returns on taxpayer funds, while Representative Steve Fitzpatrick pointed out that Montana’s investment board has substantial bank deposits that could potentially generate better returns. The bill’s sponsor, Curtis Schomer, defended the proposal by highlighting the declining purchasing power of traditional bond investments. However, these arguments failed to convince the majority of lawmakers, who viewed the investment strategy as too speculative.Montana now joins North Dakota, Wyoming, and Pennsylvania among states that have rejected Bitcoin reserve proposals. However, the movement to integrate cryptocurrencies into public finance continues to gain traction elsewhere. According to Bitcoin Reserve Tracker data, approximately 19 state proposals remain under consideration.Arizona’s Senate Finance Committee has advanced legislation that would allow up to 10% of public funds, including pension systems, to be invested in cryptocurrency. The bill is now proceeding to the Senate Rules Committee. Utah has made particular progress with its Blockchain and Digital Innovation Amendments bill, which would permit the state treasurer to allocate up to 5% of public funds to digital assets. The bill has cleared several legislative hurdles and awaits final votes.Texas is pursuing a two-pronged approach with separate bills. One proposal would allow up to 1% of the general revenue fund to be invested in Bitcoin,

data.title: "Qubetics Surges: New Web3 Aggregator Outperforms Arweave and AAVE"
data.description:
The crypto market is abuzz with innovation, and two projects, Arweave and AAVE, have been making significant strides. Arweave, known for its permanent data storage solution, is gaining traction due to its role in decentralizing information. Meanwhile, AAVE, a heavyweight in decentralized finance (DeFi), is evolving rapidly with game-changing updates that could redefine lending and borrowing in Web3. However, a new player, Qubetics ($TICS), is turning heads with its potential to outperform these established projects.

Qubetics, the world's first Web3 aggregator, is setting new industry standards by uniting the best features of major blockchains under one roof. Its QubeQode IDE is a game-changer for developers and businesses, making blockchain development as simple as building a website. The QubeQode IDE simplifies Web3 app creation, making it accessible for developers and businesses of all sizes. Enterprises, DeFi startups, and NFT projects can now leverage Qubetics as a multi-chain hub, seamlessly integrating with Ethereum, Solana, Binance Smart Chain, and more.

The Qubetics presale is smashing records, with over $13.9 million raised and more than 483 million tokens sold. Each stage of the presale lasts only 7 days, and the token price jumps by 10% every Sunday at 12 AM. Currently priced at $0.08880, analysts predict that $TICS could hit $10 or even $15 per token by the time the Q2 2025 mainnet launch arrives. This presents an investment opportunity of a lifetime, with the potential for a $10,000 investment to balloon into a jaw-dropping $1.69 million.

While Qubetics is transforming blockchain usability, Arweave is tackling one of the internet's biggest problems: permanent data storage. The project offers a decentralized, censorship-resistant solution, ensuring that data stays online forever. Major organizations have started leveraging Arweave for archiving crucial data, from government records to NFT metadata. As blockchain usage grows, Arweave's role as a data security powerhouse could make it one of the most essential infrastructures in the decentralized internet.

data.title: Western Union's EPS Surges: A Closer Look at the Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Western Union (NYSE: WU) has reported its full year 2024 earnings, with earnings per share (EPS) surpassing analyst expectations. The company's EPS for the year was $2.74, up from $1.68 in 2023, driven by a 49% increase in net income to $934.2 million. Revenue, however, decreased by 3.4% to $4.21 billion, primarily due to a decline in the Consumer Money Transfer segment.



Western Union's profit margin improved significantly, rising to 22% from 14% in 2023. This increase was primarily driven by lower expenses, as the company focused on improving marketing and technology efficiencies. The company's adjusted operating margin also increased to 19% from 20% in 2023, demonstrating its ability to maintain profitability despite the revenue decline.



The Consumer Money Transfer segment, which accounts for the majority of Western Union's revenue, saw a decline of 3.4% in 2024. However, the Consumer Services segment grew by 28% on a reported basis, or 15% on an adjusted basis, driven by new and expanded products. The Branded Digital business, part of the Consumer Services segment, contributed significantly to this growth, with revenue increasing by 7% on a reported basis, or 8% on an adjusted basis, and transaction growth of 13%.



Western Union's digital strategy has been a key driver of its growth. The company has invested in digital infrastructure and expanded its digital wallet services in Singapore and Mexico. Additionally, it has grown a new advertising business segment, further diversifying its revenue streams. These initiatives have contributed to the company's overall financial performance and growth.

Devin McGranahan, President and Chief Executive Officer of Western Union, stated, "We concluded 2024 with a solid performance, marking our third consecutive quarter of positive adjusted revenue growth, excluding Iraq, which was bolstered by 15% adjusted revenue growth in Consumer Services and the seventh consecutive quarter of double-digit transaction growth in our Branded Digital business. As we enter the final year of our Evolve 2025 strategy, I am pleased with the advancements we have achieved and the positive shift in the Company’s growth trajectory. Looking ahead, I believe we are poised to drive further improvements and efficiencies, and I am confident in our ability to continue delivering value to our investors and stakeholders."



In conclusion, Western Union's full year 2024 earnings results demonstrate the company's ability to maintain profitability and drive growth, despite a decline in its core Consumer Money Transfer segment. The company's focus on improving efficiencies, innovating, and expanding its product offerings has contributed to its overall financial performance and growth. As Western Union enters the final year of its Evolve 2025 strategy, investors can expect the company to continue delivering value and driving further improvements.
